final_answer:
Implemented properly; the three production gaps are closed.

What changed:
- Deleted the old staking-status compatibility query instead of leaving it disabled.
- Validator signer keys now must be real valid secp256k1 public keys.
- Genesis now rejects duplicate validator IDs.

Why it matters: validator admission is now cleaner native Gold Chain logic, with less old bridge-event surface left around to re-enable or exploit.

Checks passed:
- Focused staking tests passed.
- Full consensus `go test ./...` passed.
- `make build` passed.
- Final search found no remaining old staking-status query/API names in the code scope.

Tooling note: full broad proto generation initially failed because vendored `.deps` proto directories are read-only. I used targeted stake-query generation successfully. If we need full `proto-all` later, the fix is to make `.deps` writable or exclude `.deps` from proto formatting. I did not restart the chain.
